name: Unsafe Deserialization via ObjectInputStream
description: ObjectInputStream.readObject() or readUnshared() deserializes untrusted data, allowing an attacker to instantiate arbitrary classes and execute code via gadget chains.
remediation: Avoid native Java deserialization of untrusted data. Use JSON, Protocol Buffers, or other safe formats. If deserialization is unavoidable, use an ObjectInputFilter (JEP 290) to restrict allowed classes.
